NKJV.1: A Song. A Psalm of Asaph. Do not keep silent, O God! Do not hold Your peace, And do not be still, O God!
NKJV.2: For behold, Your enemies make a tumult; And those who hate You have lifted up their head.
NKJV.3: They have taken crafty counsel against Your people, And consulted together against Your sheltered ones.
NKJV.4: They have said, “Come, and let us cut them off from being a nation, That the name of Israel may be remembered no more.”
NKJV.5: For they have consulted together with one consent; They form a confederacy against You:
NKJV.6: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites; Moab and the Hagrites;
NKJV.7: Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek; Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;
NKJV.8: Assyria also has joined with them; They have helped the children of Lot. Selah
NKJV.9: Deal with them as with Midian, As with Sisera, As with Jabin at the Brook Kishon,
NKJV.10: Who perished at En Dor, Who became as refuse on the earth.
NKJV.11: Make their nobles like Oreb and like Zeeb, Yes, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
NKJV.12: Who said, “Let us take for ourselves The pastures of God for a possession.”
NKJV.13: O my God, make them like the whirling dust,  Like the chaff before the wind!
NKJV.14: As the fire burns the woods, And as the flame sets the mountains on fire,
NKJV.15: So pursue them with Your tempest, And frighten them with Your storm.
NKJV.16: Fill their faces with shame, That they may seek Your name, O LORD.
NKJV.17: Let them be confounded and dismayed forever; Yes, let them be put to shame and perish,
NKJV.18: That they may know that You, whose name alone is the LORD, Are the Most High over all the earth.

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> or Psalm<S>4210</S> of Asaph<S>623</S>. Keep not thou silence<S>1824</S>, O God<S>430</S>: hold not thy peace<S>2790</S>, and be not still<S>8252</S>, O God<S>410</S>. <sup>of Asaph: or, for Asaph</sup>

KJV.5: For they have consulted<S>3289</S> together<S>3162</S> with one consent<S>3820</S>: they are<S>3772</S> confederate<S>1285</S> against thee: <sup>consent: Heb. heart</sup>
KJV.6: The tabernacles<S>168</S> of Edom<S>123</S>, and the Ishmaelites<S>3459</S>; of Moab<S>4124</S>, and the Hagarenes<S>1905</S>;
KJV.7: Gebal<S>1381</S>, and Ammon<S>5983</S>, and Amalek<S>6002</S>; the Philistines<S>6429</S> with the inhabitants<S>3427</S> of Tyre<S>6865</S>;
KJV.8: Assur<S>804</S> also is joined<S>3867</S> with them: they have holpen<S>2220</S> the children<S>1121</S> of Lot<S>3876</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>holpen: Heb. been an arm to</sup>
KJV.9: Do<S>6213</S> unto them as unto the Midianites<S>4080</S>; as to Sisera<S>5516</S>, as to Jabin<S>2985</S>, at the brook<S>5158</S> of Kison<S>7028</S>:
KJV.10: Which perished<S>8045</S> at Endor<S>5874</S>: they became as dung<S>1828</S> for the earth<S>127</S>.
KJV.11: Make<S>7896</S> their nobles<S>5081</S> like Oreb<S>6159</S>, and like Zeeb<S>2062</S>: yea, all their princes<S>5257</S> as Zebah<S>2078</S>, and as Zalmunna<S>6759</S>:
KJV.12: Who said<S>559</S>, Let us take to ourselves the houses<S>4999</S> of God<S>430</S> in possession<S>3423</S>.
KJV.13: O my God<S>430</S>, make<S>7896</S> them like a wheel<S>1534</S>; as the stubble<S>7179</S> before<S>6440</S> the wind<S>7307</S>.
KJV.14: As the fire<S>784</S> burneth<S>1197</S> a wood<S>3293</S>, and as the flame<S>3852</S> setteth<S>3857</S> the mountains<S>2022</S> on fire<S>3857</S>;
KJV.15: So persecute<S>7291</S> them with thy tempest<S>5591</S>, and make them afraid<S>926</S> with thy storm<S>5492</S>.
KJV.16: Fill<S>4390</S> their faces<S>6440</S> with shame<S>7036</S>; that they may seek<S>1245</S> thy name<S>8034</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>.
KJV.17: Let them be confounded<S>954</S> and troubled<S>926</S> for ever<S>5703</S>; yea, let them be put to shame<S>2659</S>, and perish<S>6</S>:
KJV.18: That men may know<S>3045</S> that thou, whose name<S>8034</S> alone is JEHOVAH<S>3068</S>, art the most high<S>5945</S> over all the earth<S>776</S>.

ESV.1: O God, do not keep silence; do not hold your peace or be still, O God!
ESV.2: For behold, your enemies make an uproar; those who hate you have raised their heads.
ESV.3: They lay crafty plans against your people; they consult together against your treasured ones.
ESV.4: They say, “Come, let us wipe them out as a nation; let the name of Israel be remembered no more!”
ESV.5: For they conspire with one accord; against you they make a covenant —
ESV.6: the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites,
ESV.7: Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;
ESV.8: Asshur also has joined them; they are the strong arm of the children of Lot. Selah
ESV.9: Do to them as you did to Midian, as to Sisera and Jabin at the river Kishon,
ESV.10: who were destroyed at En-dor, who became dung for the ground.
ESV.11: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
ESV.12: who said, “Let us take possession for ourselves of the pastures of God.”
ESV.13: O my God, make them like whirling dust,  like chaff before the wind.
ESV.14: As fire consumes the forest, as the flame sets the mountains ablaze,
ESV.15: so may you pursue them with your tempest and terrify them with your hurricane!
ESV.16: Fill their faces with shame, that they may seek your name, O LORD.
ESV.17: Let them be put to shame and dismayed forever; let them perish in disgrace,
ESV.18: that they may know that you alone, whose name is the LORD, are the Most High over all the earth.

NASB.1: A Song, a Psalm of Asaph. O God, do not remain quiet; Do not be silent and, O God, do not be still.
NASB.2: For behold, Your enemies make an uproar, And those who hate You have exalted themselves.
NASB.3: They make shrewd plans against Your people, And conspire together against Your treasured ones.
NASB.4: They have said, 'Come, and let us wipe them out as a nation, That the name of Israel be remembered no more.'
NASB.5: For they have conspired together with one mind; Against You they make a covenant:
NASB.6: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites;
NASB.7: Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;
NASB.8: Assyria also has joined with them; They have become a help to the children of Lot. Selah.
NASB.9: Deal with them as with Midian, As with Sisera [and] Jabin at the torrent of Kishon,
NASB.10: Who were destroyed at En-dor, Who became as dung for the ground.
NASB.11: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb And all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
NASB.12: Who said, 'Let us possess for ourselves The pastures of God.'
NASB.13: O my God, make them like the whirling dust, Like chaff before the wind.
NASB.14: Like fire that burns the forest And like a flame that sets the mountains on fire,
NASB.15: So pursue them with Your tempest And terrify them with Your storm.
NASB.16: Fill their faces with dishonor, That they may seek Your name, O LORD.
NASB.17: Let them be ashamed and dismayed forever, And let them be humiliated and perish,
NASB.18: That they may know that You alone, whose name is the LORD, Are the Most High over all the earth.

NLT.1:
O God, do not be silent!
Do not be deaf.
Do not be quiet, O God.
NLT.2:
Don’t you hear the uproar of your enemies?
Don’t you see that your arrogant enemies are rising up?
NLT.3:
They devise crafty schemes against your people;
they conspire against your precious ones.
NLT.4:
“Come,” they say, “let us wipe out Israel as a nation.
We will destroy the very memory of its existence.”
NLT.5:
Yes, this was their unanimous decision.
They signed a treaty as allies against you —
NLT.6:
these Edomites and Ishmaelites;
Moabites and Hagrites;
NLT.7:
Gebalites, Ammonites, and Amalekites;
and people from Philistia and Tyre.
NLT.8:
Assyria has joined them, too,
and is allied with the descendants of Lot. Interlude
NLT.9:
Do to them as you did to the Midianites
and as you did to Sisera and Jabin at the Kishon River.
NLT.10:
They were destroyed at Endor,
and their decaying corpses fertilized the soil.
NLT.11:
Let their mighty nobles die as Oreb and Zeeb did.
Let all their princes die like Zebah and Zalmunna,
NLT.12:
for they said, “Let us seize for our own use
these pasturelands of God!”
NLT.13:
O my God, scatter them like tumbleweed,
like chaff before the wind!
NLT.14:
As a fire burns a forest
and as a flame sets mountains ablaze,
NLT.15:
chase them with your fierce storm;
terrify them with your tempest.
NLT.16:
Utterly disgrace them
until they submit to your name, O LORD.
NLT.17:
Let them be ashamed and terrified forever.
Let them die in disgrace.
NLT.18:
Then they will learn that you alone are called the LORD,
that you alone are the Most High,
supreme over all the earth.

WEB.1: A song. A Psalm by Asaph. God, don’t keep silent. Don’t keep silent, and don’t be still, God.
WEB.2: For, behold, your enemies are stirred up. Those who hate you have lifted up their heads.
WEB.3: They conspire with cunning against your people. They plot against your cherished ones.
WEB.4: “Come,” they say, “let’s destroy them as a nation, that the name of Israel may be remembered no more.”
WEB.5: For they have conspired together with one mind. They form an alliance against you.
WEB.6: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites; Moab, and the Hagrites;
WEB.7: Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek; Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;
WEB.8: Assyria also is joined with them. They have helped the children of Lot. Selah.
WEB.9: Do to them as you did to Midian, as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the river Kishon;
WEB.10: who perished at Endor, who became as dung for the earth.
WEB.11: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb; yes, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna;
WEB.12: who said, “Let us take possession of God’s pasture lands.”
WEB.13: My God, make them like tumbleweed; like chaff before the wind.
WEB.14: As the fire that burns the forest, as the flame that sets the mountains on fire,
WEB.15: so pursue them with your tempest, and terrify them with your storm.
WEB.16: Fill their faces with confusion, that they may seek your name, Yahweh.
WEB.17: Let them be disappointed and dismayed forever. Yes, let them be confounded and perish;
WEB.18: that they may know that you alone, whose name is Yahweh, are the Most High over all the earth.

AMP.1:
A Song. A Psalm of Asaph. Do not keep silent, O God;
Do not hold Your peace or be still, O God.
AMP.2:
For behold, Your enemies are in tumult,
And those who hate You have raised their heads [in hatred of You].
AMP.3:
They concoct crafty schemes against Your people,
And conspire together against Your hidden and precious ones.
AMP.4:
They have said, "Come, and let us wipe them out as a nation;
Let the name of Israel be remembered no more."
AMP.5:
For they have conspired together with one mind;
Against You they make a covenant—
AMP.6:
The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites,
Of Moab and the Hagrites,
AMP.7:
Gebal and Ammon and Amalek,
Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre.
AMP.8:
Assyria also has joined with them;
They have helped the children of Lot [the Ammonites and the Moabites] and have been an arm [of strength] to them. Selah.
AMP.9:
Deal with them as [You did] with Midian,
As with Sisera and Jabin at the brook of Kishon,
AMP.10:
Who were destroyed at En-dor,
Who became like dung for the earth.
AMP.11:
Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb
And all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
AMP.12:
Who said, "Let us possess for ourselves
The pastures of God."
AMP.13:
O my God, make them like whirling dust,
Like chaff before the wind [worthless and without substance].
AMP.14:
Like fire consumes the forest,
And like the flame sets the mountains on fire,
AMP.15:
So pursue them with Your tempest
And terrify them with [the violence of] Your storm.
AMP.16:
Fill their faces with shame and disgrace,
That they may [persistently] seek Your name, O LORD.
AMP.17:
Let them be ashamed and dismayed forever;
Yes, let them be humiliated and perish,
AMP.18:
That they may know that You alone, whose name is the LORD,
Are the Most High over all the earth.

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
God, don't shut me out;
don't give me the silent treatment, O God.
MSG.2:
Your enemies are out there whooping it up,
the God-haters are living it up;
MSG.3:
They're plotting to do your people in,
conspiring to rob you of your precious ones.
MSG.4:
"Let's wipe this nation from the face of the earth,"
they say; "scratch Israel's name off the books."
MSG.5:
And now they're putting their heads together,
making plans to get rid of you.
MSG.6:
Edom and the Ishmaelites,
Moab and the Hagrites,
MSG.7:
Gebal and Ammon and Amalek,
Philistia and the Tyrians,
MSG.8:
And now Assyria has joined up,
Giving muscle to the gang of Lot.
MSG.9:
Do to them what you did to Midian,
to Sisera and Jabin at Kishon Brook;
MSG.10:
They came to a bad end at Endor,
nothing but dung for the garden.
MSG.11:
Cut down their leaders as you did Oreb and Zeeb,
their princes to nothings like Zebah and Zalmunna,
MSG.12:
With their empty brags, "We're grabbing it all,
grabbing God's gardens for ourselves."
MSG.13:
My God! I've had it with them!
Blow them away!
Tumbleweeds in the desert waste,
MSG.14: charred sticks in the burned-over ground.
MSG.15: Knock the breath right out of them, so they're gasping
MSG.17:
Bring them to the end of their rope,
and leave them there dangling, helpless.
MSG.18:
Then they'll learn your name: "God,"
the one and only High God on earth.

NIV.1:
Psalm 83
A song. A psalm of Asaph.
O God, do not keep silent;
be not quiet, O God, be not still.
NIV.2:
See how your enemies are astir,
how your foes rear their heads.
NIV.3:
With cunning they conspire against your people;
they plot against those you cherish.
NIV.4:
“Come,” they say, “let us destroy them as a nation,
that the name of Israel be remembered no more.”
NIV.5:
With one mind they plot together;
they form an alliance against you—
NIV.6:
the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites,
of Moab and the Hagrites,
NIV.7:
Gebal, Ammon and Amalek,
Philistia, with the people of Tyre.
NIV.8:
Even Assyria has joined them
to lend strength to the descendants of Lot. Selah
NIV.9:
Do to them as you did to Midian,
as you did to Sisera and Jabin at the river Kishon,
NIV.10:
who perished at Endor
and became like refuse on the ground.
NIV.11:
Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb,
all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
NIV.12:
who said, “Let us take possession
of the pasturelands of God.”
NIV.13:
Make them like tumbleweed, O my God,
like chaff before the wind.
NIV.14:
As fire consumes the forest
or a flame sets the mountains ablaze,
NIV.15:
so pursue them with your tempest
and terrify them with your storm.
NIV.16:
Cover their faces with shame
so that men will seek your name, O Lord.
NIV.17:
May they ever be ashamed and dismayed;
may they perish in disgrace.
NIV.18:
Let them know that you, whose name is the Lord—
that you alone are the Most High over all the earth.

NRSVCE.1:
O God, do not keep silence;
do not hold your peace or be still, O God!
NRSVCE.2:
Even now your enemies are in tumult;
those who hate you have raised their heads.
NRSVCE.3:
They lay crafty plans against your people;
they consult together against those you protect.
NRSVCE.4:
They say, “Come, let us wipe them out as a nation;
let the name of Israel be remembered no more.”
NRSVCE.5:
They conspire with one accord;
against you they make a covenant —
NRSVCE.6:
the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites,
Moab and the Hagrites,
NRSVCE.7:
Gebal and Ammon and Amalek,
Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;
NRSVCE.8:
Assyria also has joined them;
they are the strong arm of the children of Lot. Selah
NRSVCE.9:
Do to them as you did to Midian,
as to Sisera and Jabin at the Wadi Kishon,
NRSVCE.10:
who were destroyed at En-dor,
who became dung for the ground.
NRSVCE.11:
Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb,
all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
NRSVCE.12:
who said, “Let us take the pastures of God
for our own possession.”
NRSVCE.13:
O my God, make them like whirling dust,
like chaff before the wind.
NRSVCE.14:
As fire consumes the forest,
as the flame sets the mountains ablaze,
NRSVCE.15:
so pursue them with your tempest
and terrify them with your hurricane.
NRSVCE.16:
Fill their faces with shame,
so that they may seek your name, O LORD.
NRSVCE.17:
Let them be put to shame and dismayed forever;
let them perish in disgrace.
NRSVCE.18:
Let them know that you alone,
whose name is the LORD,
are the Most High over all the earth.

NET.1: O God, do not be silent! Do not ignore us! Do not be inactive, O God!
NET.2: For look, your enemies are making a commotion; those who hate you are hostile.
NET.3: They carefully plot against your people, and make plans to harm the ones you cherish.
NET.4: They say, “Come on, let’s annihilate them so they are no longer a nation! Then the name of Israel will be remembered no more.”
NET.5: Yes, they devise a unified strategy; they form an alliance against you.
NET.6: It includes the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites,
NET.7: Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia and the inhabitants of Tyre.
NET.8: Even Assyria has allied with them, lending its strength to the descendants of Lot. (Selah)
NET.9: Do to them as you did to Midian — as you did to Sisera and Jabin at the Kishon River!
NET.10: They were destroyed at Endor; their corpses were like manure on the ground.
NET.11: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, and all their rulers like Zebah and Zalmunna,
NET.12: who said, “Let’s take over the pastures of God!”
NET.13: O my God, make them like dead thistles, like dead weeds blown away by the wind!
NET.14: Like the fire that burns down the forest, or the flames that consume the mountainsides,
NET.15: chase them with your gale winds, and terrify them with your windstorm.
NET.16: Cover their faces with shame, so they might seek you, O LORD.
NET.17: May they be humiliated and continually terrified! May they die in shame!
NET.18: Then they will know that you alone are the LORD, the sovereign king over all the earth.
